fromElson: I am always rooting for her.
I hope she knows I am proud of every small step... like, every one.
If she is done, let the machine rest and just chat for a while.
I wish the best for her, even on the weird confusing days.
Tell her, um, she does not have to understand everything at once.
She is allowed to learn by touching the real thing.
The point is that she keeps coming back.
I hope this feels less like school and more like having a seat at the table.
If today was messy, that still counts.
She can go slowly. I am still in her corner, always.
Every time she comes back, the work gets a little less strange.
If the code got loud, she can step away. It will still be here.
I want this to feel kind, but still real.
She can begin again without making, like, a big speech about it.
...noted
I am glad she showed up. Really.
She does not have to win the whole day.
A small return is still a return.
I hope she feels allowed to be new at this.
The table has space for her, even on the awkward days.
She can ask the rough version first.
If she only learned one small thing, that counts.
I am proud that she keeps touching the real work.
She can leave the hard parts here for a minute.
The code can be confusing and she can still belong.
I hope she knows the door stays open.
She is allowed to not know yet.
Tell her the little steps are not fake steps.
I am still rooting for her when it is slow.
She can come back messy. That is allowed.
The point is not to perform. The point is to return.
If she got frustrated, okay... she still came close.
I hope this keeps feeling like a place she can enter.
She can be curious before she is confident.
Every return makes the room a little more hers.
Tell her there is no exam at the door.
She can rest without losing the thread.
I want her to feel held by the process, not judged by it.
The work is allowed to meet her halfway.
She showed up, and that is not nothing.
She can learn out loud here.
I hope the machine felt less cold today.
She does not have to translate herself perfectly.
The confusing parts can stay confusing for a bit.
I am glad she keeps trying the door.
She can take the soft way back in.
A little courage is still courage.
If the day was heavy, this still counts.
She can stop before she is empty.
I hope she knows I mean it.
The next time can start small too.
She belongs before she understands everything.
The room remembers that she came back.
Tell her the work got a little less strange today.
She can trust the tiny beginnings.
I am rooting for the version of her that keeps returning.
If she is tired, she can be done.
She can just be here. That is enough for now.
The code is not more important than her.
I hope she leaves feeling a little less outside.
